div.div:
Materializing
NewWriter → RegionsForFlowWriter
→ fabrics.NewTarget → Setup
→ MarshalString

div.div:
Ready
status.targetInfo published
progress goroutine running

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 278px; height: 1px; padding-top: 365px; margin-left: 801px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0px; text-align: center;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(31, 41, 51); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; overflow-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Rebuilding fabric side
close info/target/regions
KEEP mxl.Writer
openFabricSide → new TargetInfo

div.div:
Pod restart
status persists in etcd but in-memory entry is empty.
Fast-path declines (no live entry); slow path reopens
the writer, which also rotates TargetInfo.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 270px; height: 1px; padding-top: 243px; margin-left: 1120px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0px; text-align: left;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(31, 41, 51); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; overflow-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Deletion path
DeletionTimestamp + finalizer present
→ closeEntry: stop progress goroutine, close info,
target, regions, writer
→ RemoveFinalizer

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 340px; height: 1px; padding-top: 353px; margin-left: 410px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0px; text-align: left;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(31, 41, 51); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; overflow-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Why keep mxl.Writer alive on recovery
Closing the writer would remove the on-disk flow_def
and invalidate every consumer pod's FlowReader handle.
We only rebuild the fabric side; consumers don't notice.

switch.text: Why keep mxl.Writer alive on recovery...

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 340px; height: 1px; padding-top: 483px; margin-left: 410px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0px; text-align: left;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(31, 41, 51); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; overflow-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Progress loop uses ReadGrainNonBlocking
Go's SIGURG-based preemption + libfabric's util_wait.c
EINTR handling (gated on #if ENABLE_DEBUG) tears down a
blocking ReadGrain every 10–60s. Polling sidesteps it.
On non-recoverable error → exit loop → recoverFromFatalError.
